title: 4 Choosing an OLT Environment
body_text:
4	Choosing an OLT Environment
4.1	Overview of OLT Environments
Each financial institution chooses an OLT environment that best suits its needs. The following three models are described in this Chapter:
Note:	Regardless of which OLT environment is selected, Tracers and SuperTracers can view and create trace requests for all branches and can view all biller-initiated trace requests. SuperTracers can also authorize trace requests for all branches.
Centralized Environment
â A designated branch (e.g., the head office) creates, authorizes (if applicable), and receives trace requests on OLT on behalf of the other branches. This option is suitable for multi-branch financial institutions with a regional administration centre.
Partially Centralized Environment
â Each branch creates trace requests. A designated branch (e.g., the head office) authorizes and submits the trace requests. The financial institutionâs internal policy determines if each branch or the designated branch acts on biller-initiated traces. This option is suitable for multi-branch financial institutions with a regional administration centre.
Decentralized Environment
â Each branch creates, authorizes, and receives trace requests. This option is suitable for financial institutions with one branch. Multi-branch institutions can use this model if each branch is responsible for creating, authorizing, and receiving trace requests.
4.2	Setting up a Centralized OLT Environment
In a centralized OLT environment, a designated branch (e.g., the head office) creates, authorizes (if applicable), and receives trace requests on behalf of the other branches. Since branch staff donât have access to OLT, they fax or email trace requests to the designated branch. Staff at the designated branch will enter the trace requests on OLT and monitor OLT for status changes and settlement information.
Tracers and SuperTracers can create trace requests for all branches and can view all biller-initiated trace requests.
Set up OLT users in a centralized OLT environment as described in the following table. Detailed procedures on creating Tracers and SuperTracers are provided in Section 5.2, Creating Tracers and SuperTracers. Detailed procedures on setting up the notification function are provided in Section 6.3, Setting Up Automatic Trace Notifications on OLT.
Setting Up a Centralized OLT Environment
Branch Users
Not applicable.
Designated Branch (Head Office) Users
Set up all OLT users under the head office transit number.
Create all users as SuperTracers if the financial institutionâs internal policy doesnât require authorization on new trace requests. SuperTracers can create trace requests.
Create both Tracers and SuperTracers if the financial institutionâs internal policy does require authorization on new trace requests.
Note: Create a minimum of two SuperTracers.
Notification Feature (optional)
If this feature is enabled, the specified email group will be notified of status changes to all trace requests.
4.3	Setting up a Partially Centralized OLT Environment
In a partially centralized OLT environment, each branch creates trace requests and a designated branch (e.g., head office) authorizes and submits the trace requests.
For each branch to enter trace requests on OLT, set up Tracers at each branch. However, Tracers can create trace requests on transactions that originated at any branch.
SuperTracers are set up at the designated branch (e.g., the head office) and authorize trace requests for all branches.
Since both Tracers and SuperTracers can view biller-initiated trace requests, the financial institutionâs internal policy determines whether the branches or the designated branch acts on biller-initiated trace requests.
Set up OLT users in a partially centralized OLT environment as described in the following table. Detailed procedures on creating Tracers and SuperTracers are provided in Section 5.2, Creating Tracers and SuperTracers. Detailed procedures on setting up the notification function are provided in Section 6.3, Setting Up Automatic Trace Notifications on OLT.
Setting Up a Partially Centralized OLT Environment
Branch Users
Create as Tracers and assign users to the transit number of their home branch.
Designated Branch (Head Office) Users
Create as SuperTracers and assign each user to the head office transit number.
Note: Create a minimum of two SuperTracers.
Notification Feature (optional)
The OLT notification feature can be enabled or disabled on a per branch basis.
If a different email address is provided for each branch, members of that email group are only notified of status changes to trace requests associated with that branch.
If the same email address is provided for each branch, members of that email group are notified of status changes to all traces.
4.4	Setting up a Decentralized OLT Environment
In a decentralized OLT environment, each branch creates, authorizes (if applicable), and receives its own trace requests. A designated branch (e.g., the head office) isnât involved in any aspect of trace request processing.
Set up Tracers at each branch to enter trace requests on OLT. These Tracers can create trace requests on transactions that originated at any branch.
Set up SuperTracers at each branch to authorize trace requests on OLT. Although SuperTracers can authorize trace requests for any branch, the financial institution can instruct SuperTracers to authorize only those trace requests that originate at their own branch.
Note:	Create all users as SuperTracers if the financial institutionâs internal policy doesnât require authorization on new trace requests. SuperTracers can create trace requests.
Tracers and SuperTracers can view all trace requests including biller-initiated trace requests. The financial institution can instruct Tracers and SuperTracers to process only those biller-initiated trace requests that are associated with their own branch.
Set up OLT users in a decentralized OLT environment as summarized in the following table. Detailed procedures on creating Tracers and SuperTracers are provided in Section 5.2, Creating Tracers and SuperTracers. Detailed procedures on setting up the notification function are provided in Section 6.3, Setting Up Automatic Trace Notifications on OLT.
Setting Up a Decentralized OLT Environment
Branch Users
For each branch, create all users as SuperTracers if the financial institutionâs internal policy doesnât require authorization on new trace requests. SuperTracers can create trace requests. Assign each SuperTracer to the transit number of their home branch.
Or, for each branch, create both Tracers and SuperTracers if the financial institutionâs internal policy requires authorization on new trace requests. Assign each Tracer and SuperTracer to the transit number of their home branch.
Note: Create a minimum of two SuperTracers.
Designated Branch (Head Office) Users
Not applicable.
Notification Feature (optional)
The OLT notification feature can be enabled or disabled on a per branch basis.
If a different email address is provided for each branch, members of that email group are only notified of status changes to traces associated with that branch.
If the same email address is provided for each branch, members of that group are notified of status changes to all traces.
END
